description: THE PROJECT CONSISTS OF THE STABILIZATION OF THE EXISTING BUILDING ENVELOPE AS REQUIRED FOR A WEATHERTIGHT ENCLOSURE. THIS INCLUDES BUT IS NOT LIMITED TO THE REMOVAL AND REPLACEMENT OF ALL OPENINGS, REPAIR OF MASONRY/STONE CLADDING; SEALING OF CRACKS AND PENETRATIONS; SECURING THE BUILDING; AND MITIGATING FURTHER DETERIORATION BY CONTROLLING INTERIOR HUMIDITY LEVELS. ADDITIONAL SCOPE INCLUDES THE ABATEMENT OF HAZARDOUS MATERIALS AS REQUIRED FOR THE ABOVE DESCRIBED WORK.

Renovation and addition to the existing Sixth Baptist Church located at 928 Felicity Street, New Orleans, LA. Work includes interior renovation of the existing 2,880 square foot single-story sanctuary, demolition of an existing 1,500 square foot rear structure, and construction of a new two-story rear addition totaling approximately 3,080 square feet (2,200 square feet on the first floor and 880 square feet on the second floor) to accommodate expanded sanctuary space, restrooms, and storage. The project also includes site improvements with new parking, fencing, landscaping, and sidewalks, as well as upgraded mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems throughout the facility
Constuction of a new dining hall for Trinity Episcopal School. PHASED RELEASE ISSUED 12-1-25

description: The building is an existing 18-story hotel plus basement. The project consists of infill of existing indoor swimming pool and hot tub located in the basement to provide a a level floor surface. Hot tub walls to be removed and floor infill constructed per drawings. No other work/exterior work under this permit.

Exploratory demolition of the front porch load bearing brick wall and foundation below grade plus analysis of any additional structural concerns. .After pursuing a permit through the VCC to re-point and shore up our front porch load-bearing brick wall (reference code C31KHZ), the Architecture Committee ruled that we should get a general permit to dig in just enough to get some engineering drawings. The contractor's engineer will submit these drawings to move the permit to the next stage of work. We will then take those drawings to the VCC and get approval for that permit. Per Bryan Block from VCC: "The engineer should then investigate causes of the apparent failure. This would likely include exploratory demolition of the foundation below grade and analysis of any additional structural concerns. Permits should be obtained from the Dept of Safety and Permits (DSP) for this and all work prior to commencing." We have been seeking permission to move forward since April.

description: To repair and renovate building as per plans. Work includes: Installation of three new 8” round tie backs on the Dumaine elevation. Tie backs to be painted to match the building’s stucco, sounding and removal of any damaged or missing stucco to be repaired with new stucco, repair/replacement of wood windows to match existing, installation of new steel lintel at ground floor opening and repair of surrounding stucco to match adjacent openings, repair shutters at the second and third floor of the Decatur St. elevation to match existing, removal of the rooftop mechanical unit currently servicing the neighboring building at 911 Decatur St., repair existing slate roof to match existing, at rear flat roof, coat existing TPO roofing with 100% silicone roof coating for TPO, remove light fixtures, install two new can lights to the underside of the gallery, and place all wiring in conduit.
